title: Milk Chocolate Foiled Bilby
content:

"Their distribution in the recent past was Target, Myer, and David Jones and various specialty stores including IGA but they have become rare or extinct in many locations," they wrote. If you're looking for ethical gift options this season, you might also want to explore our Easter candy Australia guide, which features a range of conscious chocolate choices for the festive period.

Why Bilbies Matter

According to Save The Bilby Fund, "Bilbies are a 'keystone' species. This means that their protection is even more important because their survival, in turn, increases the chances of 19 other threatened species who share the same habitats." Bilbies are one of nature's eco-engineers. Greater bilbies are important in the restoration of soil and rejuvenation of vegetation in arid Australia. They use their strong front paws to dig deep holes in soil that enables plant material to fall in and decompose. At the same time, soil is aerated which supports seed germination. Bilbies essentially create numerous compost pits every night.

The Pink Lady Chocolate Experience

The Pink Lady Chocolate is making rich and smooth textured experience. And chocolate is refined through a process known as conching – reducing particle sizes of both cocoa solids and sugar crystals. All Pink Lady Chocolate is conched down to just sixteen microns to ensure a smooth, premium, superfine chocolate experience. This is why we keep Pink Lady Chocolate as our Pick & Mix chocolate in-store at Westfield Kotara in Newcastle, Charlestown Square in Lake Macquire, and Stockland Greenhills in Maitland!

Frequently Asked Questions

How much of the purchase goes to Save The Bilby Fund?

30 cents from each Milk Chocolate Foiled Bilby purchase is donated to Save The Bilby Fund, helping to support the protection and conservation of this important Australian native species.

Who manufactures the Milk Chocolate Foiled Bilby?

The Milk Chocolate Foiled Bilby is made by Pink Lady, and it's the Fund's official milk chocolate Easter bilby - one of the most recognisable chocolate bilbies sold every Easter.

Why is buying a chocolate bilby better than a chocolate bunny?

By purchasing a chocolate bilby instead of an Easter bunny, you're supporting the conservation of an iconic Australian native species while your purchase directly contributes to the Save The Bilby Fund's critical work protecting bilbies and the 19 other threatened species that share their habitats.

What makes Pink Lady's milk chocolate special?

Pink Lady's milk chocolate offers a rich and smooth textured experience, refined through a process called conching that reduces the particle sizes of both cocoa solids and sugar crystals for a premium taste experience.
